decisions.title: Concern about performance
decisions.detail: There are concerns regarding the performance of the dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance.

decisions.title: Recommendation to use a better database
decisions.detail: It was mentioned that the project should probably use a better database at some point, likely to address performance concerns.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI needs aesthetic improvement
decisions.detail: The User Interface (UI) needs to be improved visually to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design is not satisfactory
decisions.detail: There was a general agreement among attendees that the current UI design is not good.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Timeline discussion inconclusive
decisions.detail: Discussions about project timelines occurred, but no firm decisions were made.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Potential future CRM system integration
decisions.detail: There is a potential future need to integrate the dashboard with the CRM system.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually

decisions.title: Uncertainty regarding CRM integration timing and method
decisions.detail: The team is currently unsure about when or how the integration with the CRM system would occur.

evidence.quote: but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget is a concern without specific figures
decisions.detail: Budget was mentioned as a concern, but no specific numbers or details were shared.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Recommendation for more thorough testing
decisions.detail: The team lead indicated that testing processes should be more thorough.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Recommendation for improved documentation
decisions.detail: There is a need to improve documentation practices going forward.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Implement visual improvements to the dashboard UI components.
Enhance and implement more thorough testing processes for the project.
Improve documentation practices for developed components and systems.
Prepare for potential future integration with a CRM system (inferred_needs_confirmation).

constraints.text: The dashboard project must address underlying performance concerns.

architectural_decisions.text: Consideration to upgrade or change database technology for performance improvement (inferred_needs_confirmation).

open_technical_questions: Evaluate and select a 'better' database solution for the dashboard, pending confirmation of D002.
Determine the technical approach (e.g., API, data sync, middleware) for potential future CRM integration, pending clarification on timing and scope (related to D006 and D007).

acceptance_criteria: Given a new UI design for the dashboard is implemented, When a user navigates to any dashboard page, Then the page displays the updated visual design as per approved specifications.

Given a user interacts with any UI component (e.g., buttons, forms, navigation elements), When the component is interacted with, Then its visual feedback and styling conform to the new approved UI design.
Given the dashboard loads data, When the data is displayed, Then performance metrics (e.g., page load time, data refresh time) meet the defined performance thresholds.
test_scenarios: Verify all key dashboard pages (e.g., Home, Reports, Settings) display the new aesthetic UI without visual regressions.
Validate the consistency of styling (fonts, colors, spacing, component appearance) across all sections of the dashboard.
Test the responsiveness of the new UI on various screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile) and orientations.
Execute performance tests to measure page load times for main dashboard views under various data loads.
Execute performance tests to measure data refresh times after applying filters or interacting with data manipulation components.
edge_cases: Verify the new UI gracefully handles extreme data values (e.g., very long text, exceptionally large numbers, zero values) in tables and charts.
Verify the UI renders correctly when using different browser types (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) and common versions.
Verify the UI's error states (e.g., data loading failures, backend errors) are displayed aesthetically and provide clear feedback according to the new design.
Verify the UI's behavior and rendering when no data is available for display (empty state).
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific visual design specifications, mockups, or style guides for the 'nicer UI' that the dashboard should adhere to?
What are the acceptable performance thresholds (e.g., maximum page load time, data refresh time, number of concurrent users) for the dashboard?
Is the CRM system integration a currently planned feature, or does it remain a future consideration? If current, what are the specific requirements (e.g., data points, integration method, timing)?

What specific types of testing (e.g., performance, security, accessibility, localization) are now explicitly required, and what are the coverage expectations to meet the 'more thorough testing' recommendation?

Does 'improved documentation' refer to user-facing documentation (e.g., help guides, tooltips) that needs to be delivered with the product, or internal technical documentation? If user-facing, what are the requirements for its content and accessibility?

decision_history: It was generally agreed that the current UI design is not satisfactory and needs aesthetic improvement (D004, D003).
It was decided that testing processes should be more thorough going forward (D009).
It was decided that documentation practices need to be improved (D010).
dependencies: Future integration with the CRM system is a potential dependency (inferred, D006).
risks: There are confirmed concerns regarding the performance of the dashboard project (D001).
Budget is a concern, though no specific figures or details were shared, making it an unquantified risk (D008).
alignment_gaps: Discussions about project timelines were inconclusive, suggesting potential for differing stakeholder expectations on delivery dates (D005).
Uncertainty persists regarding the timing and method for a potential future CRM system integration, which could lead to scope misalignment or rework (D006, D007).
open_questions: What are the agreed-upon project timelines and key delivery dates? (Impact: High - affects all planning and stakeholder expectations) (D005)

Is a database upgrade or change required to address the identified performance concerns? If so, what are the implications for project scope, timeline, and budget? (Impact: High - fundamental technical decision affecting core project delivery and resources) (D002, D001)

Is CRM system integration a definite future requirement? If so, what are the high-level functional requirements, target timing, and proposed method to inform future scope and resource planning? (Impact: Medium - affects future roadmap and potential resource allocation) (D006, D007)

system_requirements: The dashboard system SHALL exhibit improved performance capabilities to address current concerns.
The system UI SHALL be aesthetically improved.
The system development and deployment processes SHALL incorporate more thorough testing practices.
The project SHALL ensure improved documentation practices for all system components and processes.
The dashboard system SHALL support future integration capabilities with the CRM system. (inferred — needs confirmation)
data_flows: A potential future data flow exists between the Dashboard system and the CRM system for undefined data entities.
interface_contracts: No specific interface contracts are formally defined at this time. A future interface contract will be required for the CRM system integration.

constraints.text: The dashboard project MUST address existing performance concerns.
The project operates under general budget concerns.

architectural_decisions.text: The current UI design is deemed unsatisfactory and requires redesign.
It is recommended to evaluate and potentially adopt an improved database technology to address performance concerns. (inferred — needs confirmation and specific selection)

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific performance metrics (e.g., response time, throughput, concurrency limits) and target values that the dashboard system must achieve to address current concerns?
What are the functional and non-functional requirements for the aesthetic improvements to the UI, specifically in terms of design specifications, responsiveness, or accessibility standards?
What are the detailed requirements for the potential CRM system integration, including specific data entities to be exchanged, data flow direction, frequency, triggers, and expected data volumes?

If a 'better database' is required, what are the specific technical criteria for its selection (e.g., data model suitability, scalability requirements, transactional integrity, specific data access patterns) to address identified performance concerns?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has identified critical needs to improve the dashboard's performance and user interface. Key next steps involve addressing these technical and design challenges to ensure a better user experience, though specific timelines and budget details are still being worked out.
key_impact: Users may experience slow performance with the current dashboard, impacting productivity and satisfaction.
The dashboard's visual design is not meeting expectations, which could affect user adoption and overall perception of the product.
Uncertainty around project timelines and budget could delay the delivery of essential improvements.
Without clear plans for integration with our CRM system, the dashboard's future strategic value may be limited.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirm if the project should upgrade to a different database to address performance concerns.
Determine if and when the dashboard needs to be integrated with the CRM system.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the firm timelines for completing the dashboard improvements, particularly for performance and design?
What is the specific budget allocated for these enhancements?
When and how will the dashboard connect with our CRM system?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, quality issues, or sensitive content were detected. The outputs are well-structured and ready for publication.

evidence_quotes: there are some concerns about performance.
